findings: The dominant ratio of 1.0 for destination port in the DDoS class is confirmed at the global distribution level.
Local consistency checks reveal high label neighborhood conflicts and duplicate label conflicts, indicating instability of this ratio across local partitions.
These conflicts suggest potential sampling artifacts or local irregularities affecting the destination port distribution in the DDoS class.
limitations: Local evidence shows instability and conflicts, limiting confidence in ratio stability across all partitions.
